g.text: 💾 Binary code: only language directly executed by computers without translation
🔢 Written as zeros and ones (off/on electrical states)
⚠️ Drawback: Nearly impossible for humans to read/write
👤 Human-readable version of machine code using mnemonic codes
📉 Low-level: offers more control and speed than high-level languages
➡️ Step-by-step instructions with reusable code blocks
📦 Used for medium-sized applications and embedded systems
🔒 Functions treat tasks like math problems without changing values/states
✨ Uses pure functions and immutable data
🎯 Everything as objects with attributes and methods
🤖 Automates repetitive tasks and manages dynamic content
✓ Based on formal logic with facts and rules
💡 Computers solve computations through logical inference
📄 Text-encoding systems for structure, presentation, and content organization
